question.text: Under SEBI RTA Regulations, within how many calendar days must an RTA redress investor grievances?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI RTA Regulations mandate that RTAs must redress investor grievances within 21 calendar days of receiving the complaint. This is a specific timeline applicable to RTAs under their dedicated regulatory framework.
**The Trap:** The 45-day timeline is a common distractor because it applies under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008 — a different regulation. Candidates often mix up these two timelines. The 21-day rule is specific to RTAs, while 45 days is the general intermediary standard.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM Series VI (Depository Operations), similar grievance redressal timelines exist for depository participants. Knowing which timeline applies to which intermediary is a frequently tested concept across multiple NISM certifications.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set up internal tracking systems that flag complaints approaching the 15-day mark. This gives your team a buffer of 6 days to resolve complex cases before breaching the 21-day regulatory deadline. Document every step of the resolution process for audit purposes.
